service_identification:
| Service | TAM IWS (IBM Workload Scheduler) | | Unique ID | PRD.0.L.185 | | Service Model | Technical Application Management (TAM) This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatory services are called the ‘Platform’. |
Service TAM IWS (IBM Workload Scheduler) Unique ID PRD.0.L.185 Service Model Technical Application Management (TAM) This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatory services are called the ‘Platform’.
technical application management:
The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage an IWS application (The Application) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory Platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects from infrastructure up to the technical Application.
The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.  Cegeka ensures the functionalities of the Application.
The Customer is accountable to define and use the jobs within the IWS platform.
Cegeka performs the monitoring of the scheduled jobs and triggers incident management towards the Customer.
the application: IBM Workload Scheduler  (IWS)  is  a  software automation tool that provides the backbone for automated workload management and monitoring. It provides:
service_features:
This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features.
The following optional features are possible with the Service:
application knowledge: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the IWS technology in order to perform the Service.
technical_setup:
Cegeka advises in the setup of the underlaying platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Application. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants).
High availability setup: this setup advise includes also the setup of HA (High Available) environments where needed. Cegeka will advise a cost-effective solution within the context of the available platform(s) of the Customer and the best practices of the Application.
Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Application on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working.
Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer.
The service is based on Cegeka owned licences and IBM support service.
technical_management:
Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Application and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows.
The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage an IWS system from a technical perspective. The Service features have as focus to keep the systems up-and-running, to keep them secure and to optimize their technical parameters. This applies to the MDMs (Master Domain Managers), DWCs (GUI servers) and FTAs (Fault Tolerant Agents).
Cegeka provides in an Excel based tooling for easy creation and deployment of job flows by the Customer.
housekeeping:
Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible.
Proactive management
Housekeeping includes following regular checks:
Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA.
Patching
The service includes a maximum of 2 planned patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Application (inclusive the system database(s) if applicable). Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan.
Additional patching can be done on RFC basis.
Patching of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform.
Life Cycle Management
Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’.
monitoring:
Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Application on all Customer environments.
Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions.
The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform.
Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management.
security:
Management of technical users
The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies.
Detection of security incidents
The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies.
Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders.
SIEM
Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request.
Underlaying Platform hardening
The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services.
backup and restore:
The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy.
Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process.
Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored).
Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform.
The Customer defined jobs definitions are daily dumped and part of the backup.
incident and problem management:
Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals).
Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals).
customer_responsibility:
The functional usage (definition and job execution) of the Application is the responsibility of the Customer.
Job monitoring is done by Cegeka and triggers the incident process. The customer is responsible to deliver the escalation matrix for EAF for the operational handling of these job incidents (who to contact in case of a job error). Cegeka publishes the current escalation matrix.
requests:
Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). | request type | Description | Lead time | | Service Requests | Change the escalation matrix | 2 working days | | | Add a scheduling agent | 2 working days | | | Add new application (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS APP NEW) | 3 working days | | | New workstation variable (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS VARIABLE WORKSTATION) | 2 working days | | | New global variable (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS VARIABLE GLOBAL) | 2 working days | | | New variable in runcycle variable table Aanvraag nieuwe variable in een runcycle variable table (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS VARIABLE RUNCYCLE) | 2 working days | | | New resource (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS RESOURCE) | 2 working days | | | New runcycle (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS RUNCYCLE NEW) | 5 working days | | | Activation conman for local objects (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS CONMAN LOCAL) | 2 working days | | | Activation conman for global objects (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS CONMAN GLOBAL) | 2 working days | | | Lookup in IWS audit logs (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS AUDIT LOGS) | 2 working days | | | Change general holiday calendar (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS HOLIDAY) | 5 working days | | | Adjustment general holiday calendar (ARGE.STDC.MIDDLEWARE.IWS CALENDER ADJUSTMENTS) | 2 working days | | | | | | Normal Changes | Any other request. Examples: Setup new IWS environment Development new rules to support scheduling logic Development new rules to support operational process Development/changing custom SQL report Development/change custom ‘zaalverslag’ Consultancy on the use of the platform | Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request. |

delivery_location: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.
reporting:
The Cross-functional reporting is part of this Service Reporting where it is applicable for this service (see section ‘Relation to Cross-Functional Services’).
Specific reports are provided:
data_classification:
Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service.
Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service.
In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).
subcontractors:
This service is delivered with:
Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues):
IBM (for IWS licenses and IWS support)
Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party):
(none)

adherence to cross-functional services:
The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: | Cross-Functional Service | Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services | | Anti-Malware management | This service itself is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. As a result, the IWS application is also subject to Anti-Malware. | | Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) | This service itself is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. |
Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Anti-Malware management This service itself is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. As a result, the IWS application is also subject to Anti-Malware. Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) This service itself is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. All Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.
service_level:
Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. The actual mapping of the operational service instances towards these KPI’s are documented on the Service Delivery site under ‘Operational documentation’. service_window: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.
billing_change:
Changes are billed on a monthly basis.
Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact.
Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.
